title: CLAUDE.md
text: mSigPlot R package plotting mutational signatures mutational spectra including SBS (single base substitution), DBS (doublet base substitution), indel catalogs. creates publication-quality ggplot2 bar plots (heatmaps DBS136) 10 catalog types.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: plot_guess(catalog, ...) plot_guess_pdf(catalog, filename, ...) auto-detect catalog type row count dispatch appropriate function. catalog type paired plot_*() (single sample, returns ggplot) plot_*_pdf() (multi-sample PDF, 5 per page via gridExtra::grid.arrange + cairo_pdf).

title: CLAUDE.md
text: catalog_row_order() returns named list canonical row name vectors supported types (SBS96, SBS192, SBS1536, DBS78, DBS136, DBS144, ID, ID166, ID89, ID476). Strand-aware types (SBS192, DBS144) internal reorder_*_for_plotting() helpers interleave transcribed/untranscribed pairs. DBS136 uses order_DBS136_for_plotting() map 136 rows 160-position heatmap grid.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: 476-channel indel type table generated type_476_indel_type() (R/type_476_indel_type.R). 89-channel table defined inline within plot_89(). produce data frames columns: IndelType, Indel, Indel3, Figlabel.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Y-axis auto-detection: sum(catalog) < 1.1, label “Proportion”; otherwise “Count”. functions also support catalog.type attribute catalog object ("counts", "counts.signature", "density", "density.signature"). show_counts parameter: NULL (auto based catalog type), TRUE (force), FALSE (suppress). Controls per-class count labels. Text size parameters: Named *_cex (multipliers relative base_size). Deprecated *_size aliases still accepted warnings; specifying old new errors. Color palettes: Hardcoded per function consistent publication figures. PDF export: *_pdf() functions use cairo_pdf() arrange 5 plots per page (except DBS136 heatmaps: 1 per page). plot_complex parameter: Indel plots (89, 476) optional Complex channel, default.

title: Plotting mutational signatures with mSigPlot
text: mSigPlot creates publication-quality plots mutational signatures mutational spectra. supports single base substitutions (SBS), doublet base substitutions (DBS), small insertions deletions (indels) across 10 classification systems.
code: library(mSigPlot)

what: SBS96 – single base substitutions in trinucleotide context
title: Plotting mutational signatures with mSigPlot
text: 96-channel catalog one row per trinucleotide mutation context, organized 6 mutation classes (C>, C>G, C>T, T>, T>C, T>G). row names present checked agains catalog_row_order.  Row names (numeric vector, names) required. names row names sure rows order expected plotting.  plot unrecognizable.
code: sbs96_file <- system.file("extdata", "sbs96_example.csv", package = "mSigPlot") sbs96_df <- read.csv(sbs96_file) catalog_sbs96 <- data.frame(   sample1 = sbs96_df[, 3],   row.names = catalog_row_order()$SBS96 ) plot_SBS96(catalog_sbs96, plot_title = "HepG2 sample -- SBS96") plot_SBS96(sample(sbs96_df[ ,3, drop = TRUE], replace = FALSE),    plot_title = "HepG2 sample, mixed up row order -- SBS96")

title: Plotting mutational signatures with mSigPlot
text: 83-channel indel catalog uses COSMIC classification single-base multi-base deletions, insertions, microhomology deletions.
code: id83_file <- system.file("extdata", "id83_cosmic_v3.5.tsv", package = "mSigPlot") id83_sigs <- read.table(id83_file, header = TRUE, sep = "\t",                         row.names = 1, check.names = FALSE) plot_ID83(id83_sigs[, "ID1", drop = FALSE], plot_title = "COSMIC ID1 signature")

title: Plotting mutational signatures with mSigPlot
text: 89-channel system (Koh et al.) provides finer decomposition indel types, including optional complex indels.
code: id89_file <- system.file("extdata", "type89_liu_et_al_sigs.tsv",                          package = "mSigPlot") id89_sigs <- read.table(id89_file, header = TRUE, sep = "\t",                         row.names = 1, check.names = FALSE) plot_ID89(id89_sigs[, 1, drop = FALSE], plot_title = "ID89 signature")

title: Plotting mutational signatures with mSigPlot
text: 476-channel system adds flanking base context indel classification, producing detailed profile.
code: id476_file <- system.file("extdata", "type476_liu_et_al_sigs.tsv",                           package = "mSigPlot") id476_sigs <- read.table(id476_file, header = TRUE, sep = "\t",                          row.names = 1, check.names = FALSE) plot_ID476(id476_sigs[, 1, drop = FALSE], plot_title = "ID476 signature")

title: Plotting mutational signatures with mSigPlot
text: right portion 476-channel profile (positions 343–476) can plotted separately closer look multi-base indels.
code: plot_ID476_right(id476_sigs[, 1, drop = FALSE],                  plot_title = "ID476 right panel")

title: Plotting mutational signatures with mSigPlot
text: 78-channel DBS catalog covers dinucleotide substitution classes, organized 10 reference dinucleotide groups.
code: dbs78_file <- system.file("extdata", "dbs78_example.csv", package = "mSigPlot") dbs78_df <- read.csv(dbs78_file) catalog_dbs78 <- data.frame(   sample1 = dbs78_df[, 3],   row.names = paste0(dbs78_df$Ref, dbs78_df$Var) ) plot_DBS78(catalog_dbs78, plot_title = "HepG2 sample -- DBS78")

title: Plotting mutational signatures with mSigPlot
text: 192-channel catalog pairs 96 trinucleotide contexts transcribed untranscribed strand information.
code: sbs192_file <- system.file("extdata", "regress.cat.sbs.192.csv",                            package = "mSigPlot") sbs192_df <- read.csv(sbs192_file) catalog_sbs192 <- data.frame(   sample1 = sbs192_df[, 4],   row.names = catalog_row_order()$SBS192 ) plot_SBS192(catalog_sbs192, plot_title = "HepG2 -- SBS192")

title: Plotting mutational signatures with mSigPlot
text: 144-channel DBS catalog adds transcription strand context 78 dinucleotide substitution types.
code: dbs144_file <- system.file("extdata", "regress.cat.dbs.144.csv",                            package = "mSigPlot") dbs144_df <- read.csv(dbs144_file) catalog_dbs144 <- data.frame(   sample1 = dbs144_df[, 3],   row.names = paste0(dbs144_df$Ref, dbs144_df$Var) ) plot_DBS144(catalog_dbs144, plot_title = "HepG2 -- DBS144")

title: Plotting mutational signatures with mSigPlot
text: 136-channel DBS catalog displayed heatmap 10 panels (4x4 grids) rather bar chart.
code: dbs136_file <- system.file("extdata", "regress.cat.dbs.136.csv",                            package = "mSigPlot") dbs136_df <- read.csv(dbs136_file, row.names = 1) plot_DBS136(dbs136_df[, 1, drop = FALSE], plot_title = "HepG2 -- DBS136")

title: Plotting mutational signatures with mSigPlot
text: 1536-channel catalog extends trinucleotide context pentanucleotide context, displayed faceted heatmap.
code: sbs1536_file <- system.file("extdata", "regress.cat.sbs.1536.csv",                             package = "mSigPlot") sbs1536_df <- read.csv(sbs1536_file) catalog_sbs1536 <- data.frame(   sample1 = sbs1536_df[, 3],   row.names = catalog_row_order()$SBS1536 ) plot_SBS1536(catalog_sbs1536, plot_title = "HepG2 -- SBS1536")

title: Plotting mutational signatures with mSigPlot
text: 288-channel catalog adds three strand categories (transcribed, untranscribed, non-transcribed/intergenic) 96 SBS channels.
code: sbs288_file <- system.file("extdata", "SBS288_De-Novo_Signatures.txt",                            package = "mSigPlot") sbs288_df <- read.table(sbs288_file, header = TRUE, sep = "\t",                         row.names = 1, check.names = FALSE) plot_SBS288(sbs288_df[, 1, drop = FALSE], plot_title = "SBS288A") #> Scale for y is already present. #> Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale. #> Scale for y is already present. #> Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale. #> Scale for y is already present. #> Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale.

title: Plotting mutational signatures with mSigPlot
text: 166-channel indel catalog adds genic/intergenic context 83-channel COSMIC classification.
code: set.seed(42) sig_id166 <- runif(166) sig_id166 <- sig_id166 / sum(sig_id166) names(sig_id166) <- catalog_row_order()$ID166 plot_ID166(sig_id166, plot_title = "Simulated ID166 signature")

title: Plotting mutational signatures with mSigPlot
text: SBS12 plot collapses 192-channel catalog 12 bars (6 mutation classes x 2 strands) visualize transcription strand bias.
code: plot_SBS12(catalog_sbs192, plot_title = "HepG2 -- SBS12 strand bias")

title: Plotting mutational signatures with mSigPlot
text: don’t know (don’t want specify) catalog type, plot_guess() detects number rows:
code: plot_guess(catalog_sbs96, plot_title = "Auto-detected SBS96")

title: Plotting mutational signatures with mSigPlot
text: Every plot function _pdf() variant writes multi-page PDF 5 plots per page. auto-dispatch version plot_guess_pdf():
code: sbs96_mat <- as.matrix(sbs96_df[, 3:6]) rownames(sbs96_mat) <- catalog_row_order()$SBS96 colnames(sbs96_mat) <- paste0("Sample_", 1:4)  plot_guess_pdf(sbs96_mat, file.path(tempdir(), "sbs96_samples.pdf"))

title: Mutational signature plotting functions
text: Publication-quality plots mutational signatures mutational spectra. Supports SBS, DBS, indel catalogs across 10 classification systems bar charts, strand-bias plots, heatmaps.

title: Mutational signature plotting functions
code: # Install from GitHub install.packages("remotes")  # if needed remotes::install_github("steverozen/mSigPlot")

title: Mutational signature plotting functions
text: Every plot function accepts numeric vector, single-column data.frame, matrix, tibble, data.table. easiest entry point plot_guess(), auto-detects catalog type row count:
code: library(mSigPlot)  # Auto-detect and plot plot_guess(my_catalog)  # Or call a specific function plot_SBS96(sbs96_catalog, plot_title = "Sample 1")  # Export multiple samples to a multi-page PDF (5 per page) plot_guess_pdf(multi_sample_catalog, "output.pdf")

title: Mutational signature plotting functions
text: Every plot function except plot_SBS288 corresponding _pdf() variant (e.g., plot_SBS96_pdf()) writes multi-sample PDFs 5 plots per page.

text: catalog Numeric vector, single-column data.frame, matrix, tibble, data.table. number rows (length) determines plotting function used: 1536 rows: calls plot_SBS1536() 476 rows: calls plot_ID476() 192 rows: calls plot_SBS192() 166 rows: calls plot_ID166() 144 rows: calls plot_DBS144() 136 rows: calls plot_DBS136() 96 rows: calls plot_SBS96() 89 rows: calls plot_ID89() 83 rows: calls plot_ID83() 78 rows: calls plot_DBS78() plot_guess_pdf(), matrix data frame one column per sample. title: plot_guess, plot_guess_pdf — guess_plots
text: plot_guess() returns ggplot object. plot_guess_pdf() returns NULL invisibly (called side effect creating PDF file).

title: plot_guess, plot_guess_pdf — guess_plots
text: plot_guess() plots single sample. plot_guess_pdf() creates multi-page PDF file containing plots multiple samples, arranged 5 samples per page (except heatmap types: 1 per page). Uses Cairo high-quality PDF rendering.

title: plot_guess, plot_guess_pdf — guess_plots
code: # Auto-detect a 96-channel catalog and dispatch to plot_SBS96 set.seed(1) sig <- runif(96) sig <- sig / sum(sig) names(sig) <- catalog_row_order()$SBS96 plot_guess(sig, plot_title = "Auto-detected SBS96")   if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ sig <- matrix(runif(96 * 3), nrow = 96) rownames(sig) <- catalog_row_order()$SBS96 colnames(sig) <- paste0("Sig", 1:3) plot_guess_pdf(sig, filename = "auto_detected.pdf") } # }
